questions.question: If x: y = 5: 4, then find the value of (7x+y)/(6x-2y)?
If certain number is first increased by 20% and decreased by 40% and again increased by 20%, then what will be the percentage change in the number?
questions.explanation: Let number be N Number after increasing 20% = (1.2N) Number after decreasing 40% = (0.6) x (1.2) x N Number after increasing again 20% = (1.2) x (0.6) x (1.2) x N = 1.44 x 0.6 x N = 0.864N = 86.4% x N So, it is decreased by 13.6%.

questions.question: Find the ratio of total number of Reynold pens sold by shop S to the total number of Rotomac pens sold by shop P.
questions.explanation: Total pens sold by shop P = 1200 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop P = 30% of 1200 = 360 Total Reynold pens sold by shop P = 80% of 360 = 288 Total remaining pens sold by shop P = 1200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Total pens sold by shop Q = 760 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop Q = 55% of 760 = 418 Total Reynold pens sold by shop Q = 50% of 418 = 209 Total remaining pens sold by shop Q = 760 - (418 + 209) = 133 Similarly, we can calculate other values as well... ShopsTotal Pens SoldSold Rotomac pensSold Reynolds pensSold Remaining Pens P120030% of 1200 = 36080% of 360 = 2881200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Q76055% of 760 = 41850% of 418 = 209760 - (418 + 209) = 133 R64050% of 640 = 32075% of 320 = 240640 - (320 + 240) = 80 S108075% of 1080 = 81030% of 810 = 2431080 - (810 + 243) = 27 T96025% of 960 = 24090% of 240 = 216960 - (240 + 216) = 504 Total4640214811961296 Total number of Reynold pens sold by shop S = 243 Total number of Rotomac pens sold by shop P = 360 Ratio = 243: 360 = 27: 40

questions.question: Find the total number of pens sold by shops P and R together of brands other than Reynolds and Rotomac.
questions.explanation: Total pens sold by shop P = 1200 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop P = 30% of 1200 = 360 Total Reynold pens sold by shop P = 80% of 360 = 288 Total remaining pens sold by shop P = 1200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Total pens sold by shop Q = 760 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop Q = 55% of 760 = 418 Total Reynold pens sold by shop Q = 50% of 418 = 209 Total remaining pens sold by shop Q = 760 - (418 + 209) = 133 Similarly, we can calculate other values as well... ShopsTotal Pens SoldSold Rotomac pensSold Reynolds pensSold Remaining Pens P120030% of 1200 = 36080% of 360 = 2881200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Q76055% of 760 = 41850% of 418 = 209760 - (418 + 209) = 133 R64050% of 640 = 32075% of 320 = 240640 - (320 + 240) = 80 S108075% of 1080 = 81030% of 810 = 2431080 - (810 + 243) = 27 T96025% of 960 = 24090% of 240 = 216960 - (240 + 216) = 504 Total4640214811961296 Total number of pens sold by shop P of brands other than Reynolds and Rotomac = 552 Total number of pens sold by shop R of brands other than Reynolds and Rotomac = 80 Required sum = 552 + 80 = 632

questions.question: Find that total Reynold pens sold by shop P is how much per cent more than the total Rotomac pens sold by shop T?
questions.explanation: Total pens sold by shop P = 1200 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop P = 30% of 1200 = 360 Total Reynold pens sold by shop P = 80% of 360 = 288 Total remaining pens sold by shop P = 1200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Total pens sold by shop Q = 760 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop Q = 55% of 760 = 418 Total Reynold pens sold by shop Q = 50% of 418 = 209 Total remaining pens sold by shop Q = 760 - (418 + 209) = 133 Similarly, we can calculate other values as well... ShopsTotal Pens SoldSold Rotomac pensSold Reynolds pensSold Remaining Pens P120030% of 1200 = 36080% of 360 = 2881200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Q76055% of 760 = 41850% of 418 = 209760 - (418 + 209) = 133 R64050% of 640 = 32075% of 320 = 240640 - (320 + 240) = 80 S108075% of 1080 = 81030% of 810 = 2431080 - (810 + 243) = 27 T96025% of 960 = 24090% of 240 = 216960 - (240 + 216) = 504 Total4640214811961296 Total Reynold pens sold by shop P = 288 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop T = 240 Required per cent = [(288 - 240)/240] * 100 = 20%

questions.question: Find the number of pens sold by shop T of brands other than Rotomac and Reynolds.
questions.explanation: Total pens sold by shop P = 1200 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop P = 30% of 1200 = 360 Total Reynold pens sold by shop P = 80% of 360 = 288 Total remaining pens sold by shop P = 1200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Total pens sold by shop Q = 760 Total Rotomac pens sold by shop Q = 55% of 760 = 418 Total Reynold pens sold by shop Q = 50% of 418 = 209 Total remaining pens sold by shop Q = 760 - (418 + 209) = 133 Similarly, we can calculate other values as well... ShopsTotal Pens SoldSold Rotomac pensSold Reynolds pensSold Remaining Pens P120030% of 1200 = 36080% of 360 = 2881200 - (360 + 288) = 552 Q76055% of 760 = 41850% of 418 = 209760 - (418 + 209) = 133 R64050% of 640 = 32075% of 320 = 240640 - (320 + 240) = 80 S108075% of 1080 = 81030% of 810 = 2431080 - (810 + 243) = 27 T96025% of 960 = 24090% of 240 = 216960 - (240 + 216) = 504 Total4640214811961296 Number of pens sold by shop T of brands other than Rotomac and Reynolds = 504

questions.question: If 5x3+4x2+3x+2 = 0, what is the value of (1+x2)(5x+4)/(x+1)?
How many numbers between 573 and 990 are divisible by either both 3 and 7 or both 5 and 7?
questions.explanation: Numbers which are divisible by 3 and 7 is the LCM of 3 and 7. Multiple of 21 between 573 and 990 = 588, 609, ........ 987 Total numbers = [(987 - 588)/21] + 1 = 19 + 1 = 20 Numbers which are divisible by 5 and 7 is the LCM of 5 and 7. Multiple of 35 between 573 and 990 = 595, 630, ......... 980 Total numbers = [(980 - 595)/35] + 1 = 11 + 1 = 12 Numbers which are divisible by 3, 5 and 7 is the LCM of 3, 5 and 7. Multiple of 105 between 573 and 990 = 630, 735, 840, 945 Numbers which are divisible by either 3 and 7 or 5 and 7 = 20 + 12 - 4 = 28

questions.question: If a number when divided by 65 leaves remainder 47, then what will be the remainder when the same number is divided by 13?
questions.explanation: Number when divided by 65 leaves remainder 47. Let number = (65n + 47) Number when divided by 13- = (65n + 47)/13 = = (65n/13) + (47/13) When 65n is divided by 13 it leaves '0' remainder. Remainder when (65n + 47) is divided by 13 = 8

questions.question: If x = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3758/image002.png) , what is the value of x + 1/x?
questions.explanation: x = ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p3758/image002.png) => x = 1 + 1/x => (x - 1/x)2 = 12 => x2 - 2 + 1/x2 = 1 => x2 + 1/x2 = 3 => (x + 1/x)2 - 2 = 3 => (x + 1/x)2 = 5 => x + 1/x = √5 x + 1/x ≠ −√5, as all the terms in the continued fraction are positive.

questions.question: If x = 3 − 2√2, what is the value of x + 1/x?
In the given figure, O is the center of the circle. If <POS = 150° and <RPQ = 40°, what is <TSQ? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/55623/image003.jpg)
questions.explanation: Join SQ ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/55623/image004.jpg) < RSQ = 180° - 40° = 140° (opposite angles in cyclic quadrilateral) < TSQ = 180° - 140° = 40° (linear pair)

questions.question: A flask is filled with 20% acid. 49 ml of that solution is taken out and is replaced with 40% acid to make it a solution of 27% acid. Find the initial volume of solution in the flask.
questions.explanation: Suppose there was N ml of solution present in the flask. Vol of acid = 0.2N Acid removed = 49 x 0.2 = 9.8ml Acid added from the new solution = 49 x 0.4 = 19.6ml 0.2N - 9.8 + 19.6 = 0.27N => 0.07N = 9.8 => N = 140

questions.question: Tan 75° x Sin 15° = ?

A cylindrical tube open at both ends is made of metal. The internal diameter of the tube is 11.2 cm and its length is 21 cm. The metal everywhere is 0.4 cm thick. The volume of the metal (take p= 22/7) is
questions.explanation: Internal radius (r1) = 11.2/2=5.6 cm External radius (r2) = 5.6 + 0.4 = 6 cm Volume of the metal = pr22 h- pr12 h = p(r22- r12)h = p(r2 - r1)(r2+r1)h = 22/7 X (6+5.6)(6-5.6) X 21 = 22/7 X 11.6 X 0.4 X 21 = 306.24 cm3

questions.question: The ratio between the angles of a quadrilateral is 3: 4: 6: 5. Two-third the largest angle of the quadrilateral is equal to the smaller angle of a parallelogram. What is the value of adjacent angle of the parallelogram?
questions.explanation: 3x + 4x + 6x + 5x = 360° x = 20° Largest angle of quadrilateral = 6x = 6 * 20 = 120° Smallest angle of parallelogram = 120° X 2/3 = 80° So, adjacent angle = 100°

questions.question: The maximum value of 3 Sin x - 4 Cos x is
questions.explanation: 3 Sin x - 4 Cos x = + √ (32+ 42 ) Note: [ for min. use - , for max. use + ] = √25 = 5

questions.question: Some students are to be divided into two groups A and B of 10 and 6 respectively. The average percent marks obtained by the students of group A is 75 and the average percent marks of all the 16 students is 76. What are the average percent marks of children of group B?

A man rows to a place which is 40 km away and returns in 6 hours, 30 minutes. He can row 8 km with the stream in the same time as 5 km against the stream. If the speed of the stream increases by 50%, then approximately how much time will the man take to make the round trip?
questions.explanation: Let the man take 'h' hours to row 8km downstream. Hence, Downstream speed = 8/h Upstream speed = 5/h Total time taken = 40/(8/h) + 40/(5/h) = 6.5 or 5h + 8h = 6.5 or 13h = 6.5 or h = 0.5 Hence, Downstream speed = 16 km/hour Upstream speed = 10 km/hour Speed of stream = (16 - 10)/2 = 3 km/hour New speed of stream = 4.5 km/hour New Downstream speed = 17.5 km/hour New Upstream speed = 8.5 km/hour Now, time taken will be = 40/17.5 + 40/8.5 = 80/35 + 80/17 = 4160/595 = 6.99 hours or 7 hours (approx.)

questions.question: A shopkeeper allows a discount of 10% on the marked price of an item but charges a sales tax of 8.5% on the discounted price. If the customer pays ₹8300.25 as the price including the sales tax, then what is, the marked price of an item?
questions.explanation: Let the marked price on item = ₹100 Discount price = 10% = ₹10 Discounted price = ₹90 Sales tax = 8.5% of 90 = 7.65 ∴ Total paid amount = 90 + 7.65 = ₹97.65 Now, when ₹8300.25 is paid, then the marked price = (100/97.65) x 8300.25 = ₹8500

questions.question: 12 Males complete a piece of work in 4 days. 15 females complete same work in 4 days. 6 male start the work, after 2 days all males left the work. How many females are required to finish the remaining work in 3 days?
questions.explanation: Work done by 12 males in 4 days = work done by 15 females in 4 days => 12 M x 4 = 15 F x 4 => 4 M = 5 F => 1 M = 5/4 F Let no. of females = n So, (6M x 2) + (nF x 3) = 15F x 4 => (6 (5/4 F) x 2) + (nF x 3) = 15 F x 4 => 15 F + (nF x 3) = 15 F x 4 => 15 + 3n = 60 => n = 15

questions.question: In the given figure PQR is an isosceles triangle in which PQ = PR. A circle through Q touches PR at its mid-point S and intersects PQ at M, then which of the following is correct? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/33429/image001.jpg)
questions.explanation: By tangent secant theorem PS² = PM * PQ (1/2 PR)² = PM * PQ (1/2 PQ)² = PM * PQ PM = PQ/4

questions.question: Revati deposited Rs 7500 in a bank which offers compound interest rate of 6.25% per annum but the interest is compounded once in two years. How much approx. amount will Revati get after 4 years?
questions.explanation: Effective interest rate in this case = 6.25 X 2 = 12.5% Effective time cycle = 4/2 = 2 years Hence total amount = 7500 (1 + 12.5/100)2 = 7500 x 9/8 x 9/8 = Rs 9492.19 ≈ Rs 9500

questions.question: If r sin q = 1, r cos q = √3, then the value of (√3 tan q + 1) is
questions.explanation: r sin q = 1 ..(i) r cos q = √3 ..(ii) dividing (i) by (ii), (sin q/cos q) = 1/√3 So, √3 tan q + 1 = 1 + 1 = 2
